response.justification: Sarah Smith is a highly capable full-stack and distributed-systems engineer, but the role demands sustained, deep C++ expertise she does not possess. Her only documented C++ work is a six-month engagement at National Instruments in 2014–2015 writing components for a C IDE, plus a university thesis project involving wave field synthesis — neither demonstrates STL proficiency, multi-threaded system design, or secure development lifecycle knowledge. The role's industrial automation context, IEC 62443-4-1 compliance requirements, and industrial protocol stack (OPC UA, Modbus, MQTT) are entirely absent from her background, making her a poor fit regardless of her general engineering quality.
strengths: Strong high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby using async I/O and real-time data pipelines
Demonstrated event-driven architecture and platform design through the open-source GISCollective project
Full-stack versatility (TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS CDK) partially satisfies the advantageous skills section of the JD
concerns: C++ experience is limited to roughly six months in 2014–2015 and a thesis project — insufficient for a senior C++ architecture role
Zero industrial automation, OT security, or IEC 62443 exposure; entire career is in web, mobile, and SaaS domains
No experience with any required industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
